text: i play this game on my friend's gameboy color but when I came back to him he's gameboy was broken )( but i download that game and emulator  and i play it every time because its funny
author: @mrgame221

text: this is one of the few mario songs that you can truly mosh to.  all we need now is for one of the VGM bands out there to do this song.
author: @evilmidget

text:
Yeah I had a feeling the OST I got had the percussion cut off randomly.

Also I didn't notice any lag, it flowed very nicely... maybe you just did the job very well of hiding it XD

aaand yeah they did sound quite weird, though now I have no idea what I was listening to and whether it was exactly the same song :X. lol

Still, sounds great :P
author: @Christeriously

text:
Thanks, the weirdest thing with this song is it actually inserts barely tangible pauses in each of the instrument tracks at certain points, which only presented itself as a problem at the start of file 4 -- I had to insert an extra note to compensate for the lag, but fortunately it flows pretty well.

Also the, er, percussion... whatever the piranhas are, was hard to simulate at all because it constantly pans left and right and stuff MPC can't do. It never cuts out in the GBS rip I have, though.
author: @MediaMunkee

text:
This is superb :D The arpeggios sound perfect, and the instrument choice is pretty good, though I think the marios should still be accompanied by the gameboys, and the guitars are a weird choice but harps wouldn't cut off, so they seem like the better choice :P.

Also in the OST I found it seems the percussion disappears for a bit o_o in the middle of file 3... though maybe I found a different one, I dunno :P

5/5 anyway
author: @Christeriously
